task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 450026 PHD PBA temperature too high

abstract.p: 450026 PHD (Printhead) PBA temperature too high (W1, M3, Y4, K5, C6).
Reboot of the engine will help to recover from this error but it will happen again shortly after you start printing

section.title: Background info
section.p: The ASICS on the PBA of the printhead are being cooled by airflow. If the airflow is not present the temperature will rise during use (mainly during printing).
When the temperature has risen above 100°C this MRE is booked.
Most common cause of this MRE is the lack of airflow. This can be a malfunctioning of the fan in the airchannel or a blocked airflow due to pollution of the heat-sink of the printhead itself.

section.title: Supporting data
section.p: Not yet supported. If advanced technical view is available, the determination of the printheads at risk can be done.

section.title: Analyze/Repair
section.p: Ad 1) Before visiting the customer can help preparing the visit. When the top cover is opened there is an airflow detectable from the backside of the printhead carriage. Air should flow towards the user. If the fan is polluted by foam (see picture below) this in an indication the fan is not working well and the air-channel assy needs to be replaced. In all other cases the pollution of the heat-sink of the print-heads needs to checked/removed.
Ad 2) When no airflow is detected or the fan is polluted the airchannel assy needs to be replaced.

p.xref: 05 How to remove the air channel assembly

section.p: Ad 3) Check/clean pollution of heatsink 04 How to clean the printhead heatsink

section.title: Background info
section.p: The ASICS on the PBA of the printhead are being cooled by airflow. If the airflow is not present the temperature will rise during use (mainly during printing). When the temperature has risen above 100°C this MRE is booked. Most common cause of this MRE is the lack of airflow. This can be a malfunctioning of the fan in the airchannel or a blocked airflow due to pollution of the heatsink of the printhead itself.

section.title: Evaluate/Prevention
section.p: Pollution of the heatsink is mainly caused by the environment the engine is located. The pollution can build up in several month depending on the environment. Keep the air around the engine clean will help preventing the need of manual cleaning of the printheads.
Degradation of the foam around the fan of the airchannel cannot be prevented.
